raw_consumer_statement: I purchased an airline ticket for a convention I needed to be at in XXXX. There was an offer for a discount on the ticket if I applied for their credit card. I got the card and purchased the ticket, for a total charge on the card of {$2200.00}. I later lost the card that was sent to me and requested a new one. The customer service agent asked me about a balance transfer. I specifically stated that the only charge on the account was the airline ticket I had purchased, stating that should be the only charge on the account. The agent then filed a dispute, that I did not request, with the airline resulting in my ticket being cancelled, without my knowledge or consent. I was made aware of this only days before my scheduled trip when I received a notification that my credit card balance was now XXXX. I reached out to both the credit card and the airline, both of whom blamed the other party for the problem. I was forced to purchase another ticket, then change that flight, get a bit of a refund to the card from the airline and finally was rescheduled back to my original flight. The one I agreed to pay {$2200.00} for. Since then I have been trying to get this settled with the credit card company. I have paid nearly the total amount of the original {$2200.00} for my flight yet my balance is still almost {$4000.00}. For a {$2200.00} flight. I have spoken with multiple agents, over many months and it seems each time I do, my balance increases. This has been a very distressing situation. The credit card agent made the mistake and yet the credit card company is charging me three times the value of the ticket I purchased.
